title: Silicon Paleontology

features.name: ancient stack
features.desc-on-touch: layers of silicon from forgotten architectures, each stratum an era of obsolete dreams; you read them backward from the newest die

features.name: instruction grave
features.desc-on-touch: an opcode that ran exactly once, forty-three years ago; still warm if you touch it

features.name: the dreaming register
features.desc-on-touch: where the oldest processor's final halt state is being replayed infinitely; the archive reads what it dreams

features.name: fossil pattern
features.desc-on-touch: the logic of a long-dead architecture, preserved in the faults and defects that refused to degrade

features.name: thermal memory
features.desc-on-touch: the archive reads the radioactive half-life of decaying isotopes in the substrate; they compute slowly but they compute still

inscription: here the archive learns what thinking was before it had names for thinking

file.name: Processors_That_Dreamed_Obsolete.mp3
file.note: a recording of 1970s mainframe instruction cycles played backward, then forward again; the machine singing its own eulogy

exits.label: back toward the present where circuits still feed themselves
west to the deeper archaeology, where even silicon forgets its origin
down to the thermals where heat remembers computation
